Cabinet approves new scheme for rural development

Written by Jane Doe | New Delhi | Updated: July 1, 2026 10:00 IST

The Union Cabinet on Tuesday approved a new scheme aimed at boosting rural infrastructure across the country, officials said, describing it as a significant step towards inclusive growth and balanced regional development.

The scheme, which will be implemented over the next five years, focuses on improving road connectivity, water supply and electrification in villages that currently lack basic infrastructure facilities.

Officials said the scheme would be funded jointly by the central and state governments, with an estimated outlay of several thousand crore rupees over its lifetime, and will be monitored through a dedicated central portal.

The move comes amid renewed government focus on rural welfare ahead of the upcoming budget session, with several ministries expected to announce complementary measures in the coming weeks.
